result.markdown:
--- title: "AI Financial Analyst — Parte 1: Data Layer" author: "Simone Boesso" date: "2026-05-26" categories: [ai-finance, python, data] description: "Costruiamo il primo modulo: scarico dati fondamentali con yfinance e li salviamo in locale." execute: warning: false --- ## Obiettivo Il Data Layer è il fondamento del sistema. Il suo compito è recuperare bilanci, cash flow e prezzi storici per qualsiasi ticker e salvarli in locale per evitare rate limiting durante lo sviluppo. ## Installazione ::: {#0ec0ece0 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} pip install yfinance pandas ``` ::: ## Scaricare i dati base ::: {#5b972622 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import yfinance as yf import pandas as pd TICKER = "AAPL" ticker = yf.Ticker(TICKER) info = ticker.info print(f"Azienda: {info['shortName']}") print(f"Settore: {info['sector']}") print(f"Market Cap: ${info['marketCap']:,.0f}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Azienda: Apple Inc. Settore: Technology Market Cap: $4,528,552,280,064 ``` ::: ::: ## Income Statement ::: {#3a059cc6 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} income = ticker.financials.T income[["Total Revenue", "Net Income", "EBITDA"]].head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
Total Revenue Net Income EBITDA
2025-09-30 4.161610e+11 1.120100e+11 1.447480e+11
2024-09-30 3.910350e+11 9.373600e+10 1.346610e+11
2023-09-30 3.832850e+11 9.699500e+10 1.258200e+11
2022-09-30 3.943280e+11 9.980300e+10 1.305410e+11
2021-09-30 NaN NaN NaN
``` ::: ::: ## Balance Sheet ::: {#5b14b9c3 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} balance = ticker.balance_sheet.T balance[["Total Assets", "Total Debt", "Stockholders Equity"]].head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
Total Assets Total Debt Stockholders Equity
2025-09-30 3.592410e+11 9.865700e+10 7.373300e+10
2024-09-30 3.649800e+11 1.066290e+11 5.695000e+10
2023-09-30 3.525830e+11 1.110880e+11 6.214600e+10
2022-09-30 3.527550e+11 1.324800e+11 5.067200e+10
2021-09-30 NaN NaN NaN
``` ::: ::: ## Cash Flow ::: {#99ecaab7 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} cf = ticker.cashflow.T cf[["Operating Cash Flow", "Free Cash Flow", "Capital Expenditure"]].head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
Operating Cash Flow Free Cash Flow Capital Expenditure
2025-09-30 1.114820e+11 9.876700e+10 -1.271500e+10
2024-09-30 1.182540e+11 1.088070e+11 -9.447000e+09
2023-09-30 1.105430e+11 9.958400e+10 -1.095900e+10
2022-09-30 1.221510e+11 1.114430e+11 -1.070800e+10
2021-09-30 NaN NaN NaN
``` ::: ::: ## Prezzi storici (5 anni) ::: {#3ccab4a1 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} prices = ticker.history(period="5y") prices[["Close", "Volume"]].tail() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
Close Volume
Date
2026-05-19 00:00:00-04:00 298.970001 42243600
2026-05-20 00:00:00-04:00 302.250000 38229800
2026-05-21 00:00:00-04:00 304.989990 42965100
2026-05-22 00:00:00-04:00 308.820007 43627900
2026-05-26 00:00:00-04:00 308.329987 41880194
``` ::: ::: ## Salvare in locale Per non chiamare le API ad ogni render, salviamo tutto in parquet: ::: {#ad0ffe87 .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} import os os.makedirs("data", exist_ok=True) income.to_parquet(f"data/{TICKER}_income.parquet") balance.to_parquet(f"data/{TICKER}_balance.parquet") cf.to_parquet(f"data/{TICKER}_cashflow.parquet") prices.to_parquet(f"data/{TICKER}_prices.parquet") print("Dati salvati in data/") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Dati salvati in data/ ``` ::: ::: ## Prossimi passi Nel prossimo post useremo questi dati per calcolare le metriche fondamentali: ROE, ROIC, FCF yield, P/E, EV/EBITDA.
